Intro: The Playfair Cipher is a classical cryptographic algorithm invented by Charles Wheatstone in 1854, which encrypts messages using letter pairs (digrams/bigrams) and a key matrix
Step 1 Encrypt: Each letter J will be replaced with the letter I
Step 2 Encrypt: The text will be divided into pairs of 2 letters. If the total number of letters is odd, the last letter will be padded with X.
Step 3 Encrypt: The encryption process is performed repeatedly for every 2-letter pair, producing several possible patterns.
Step 1 Decrypt: The text will be divided into pairs of 2 letters.
Step 2 Decrypt: The decryption process is performed repeatedly for every 2-letter pair, producing several possible patterns.
Rectangle Encrypt Description: This occurs when the two input letters are in different rows and columns (R1 ≠ R2 and C1 ≠ C2). The first encrypted letter is taken from position (R1, C2), while the second encrypted letter is taken from position (R2, C1).
Rectangle Decrypt Description: This occurs when the two input letters are in different rows and columns (R1 ≠ R2 and C1 ≠ C2). The first decrypted letter is taken from position (R1, C2), while the second decrypted letter is taken from position (R2, C1).
Same Row Encrypt Description: This condition occurs when the two input letters are in the same row (R1 = R2) but different columns (C1 ≠ C2). The encrypted letters are obtained from the columns to the right of each input letter. If a letter is in the rightmost column, it wraps around to the leftmost column.
Same Row Decrypt Description: This condition occurs when the two input letters are in the same row (R1 = R2) but different columns (C1 ≠ C2). The decrypted letters are obtained from the columns to the left of each input letter. If a letter is in the leftmost column, it wraps around to the rightmost column.
Same Column Encrypt Description: This condition occurs when the two input letters are in the same column (C1 = C2) but different rows (R1 ≠ R2). The encrypted letters are obtained from the rows below each input letter. If a letter is in the bottom row, it wraps around to the top row.
Same Column Decrypt Description: This condition occurs when the two input letters are in the same column (C1 = C2) but different rows (R1 ≠ R2). The decrypted letters are obtained from the rows above each input letter. If a letter is in the top row, it wraps around to the bottom row.
Text even length: Text must have an even length
and cannot have same pair of 2 letter: and cannot have same pair of 2 letter
Text cannot have same pair of 2 letter: Text cannot have same pair of 2 letter
